title: Publish endpoint can reactivate held or closed assignments
reasoning: The route gates publishability only on creationStatus, then unconditionally writes status='active'. Because the same row has a separate workflow status, any assignment that is already on hold or closed but still has creationStatus='review_ready' will be reactivated through this publish route instead of requiring the appropriate resume/reopen lifecycle path.
reproduction: Create or update an assignment so creationStatus is review_ready and status is closed or hold, then POST /api/assignments/:id/publish with a valid manager principal; the final update sets status to active.
recommendation: Reject non-publishable workflow statuses before publishing, allowing only draft and optionally active for idempotency. Also include the allowed current status in the update predicate so a concurrent status change cannot be overwritten.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The feature metadata lists no linked tests, and the route has no visible assertion that closed or held assignments are rejected.
suggestedRegressionTest: Exercise POST /api/assignments/:id/publish with status='closed' and creationStatus='review_ready' and assert a 409 response plus no status change.
minimumFixScope: Add workflow-status validation and a guarded update predicate in app/api/assignments/[id]/publish/route.ts.

history.note: Fixed manually: publish now rejects non-draft/non-active workflow states and guards the final update; covered by tests/api/assignments-publish-route.test.ts.
